title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
This data model provides a standardized schema for describing artworks, objects, and items within exhibition contexts in the Linked Open Exhibition ecosystem. It is aligned with three major sector standards:

CIDOC CRM (Conceptual Reference Model) v7.1.3
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform
Wikidata - WikiProject Exhibitions

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
This data model provides a standardized schema for describing artworks, objects, and items within exhibition contexts in the Linked Open Exhibition ecosystem. It is aligned with three major sector standards:

CIDOC CRM (Conceptual Reference Model) v7.1.3
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform
Wikidata - WikiProject Exhibitions

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
section: Example: Hercules and Omphale by Lucas Cranach the Elder
text:
Example: Hercules and Omphale by Lucas Cranach the Elder
Title: Hercules and Omphale
Creator: Lucas Cranach the Elder (Q191748)
Instance of: Painting (Q3305213)
Inception: 16th century
Current Location: Kunsthistorisches Museum, Vienna (Q160112)
Collection: Kunsthistorisches Museum
Main Subject: Hercules and Omphale (Q117010959) / Greek mythology
Material: Oil on wood panel
Width: 210 cm
Height: 160 cm
Inventory Number: [Museum catalog number]
Image: [Wikimedia Commons: Hercules and Omphale by Lucas Cranach]
Described at: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Paintings_of_Hercules_and_Omphale_by_Lucas_Cranach_(I)

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
Best Practices for Item Records

Creator Attribution: Always link to the artist’s Wikidata item (P170) to enable comprehensive artist profiles and work discovery.
Materials Specification: Use standardized material items (oil, canvas, bronze) rather than free text to enable material-based collection browsing and conservation research.
Dimensions: Record all three dimensions (width, height, depth) with consistent units (typically cm for smaller works, m for large installations) to support condition assessments and exhibition planning.
Exhibition History Documentation: Record all known exhibitions where the item has been displayed (P1431) with corresponding dates to support provenance research and exhibition network analysis.
Provenance Chain: Document the complete ownership history (P2670, P127) in chronological order from creation through current ownership. Flag items with gaps or uncertain provenance for curatorial review.
Acquisition Records: Link acquisition events (P1830) with date entered collection (acquisition dates) to establish clear accession points and support repatriation research where applicable.
Subject Classification: Use P921 for primary subject and P180 for depicted entities to enable thematic searching across exhibitions.
Inventory Standards: Maintain distinct inventory numbers (P217) and catalog codes (P528) to distinguish between institutional accession records and publication references.
Image Uploads: Always upload high-quality images to Wikimedia Commons (P18) to provide visual context and enhance global knowledge sharing.
Collection Cross-Reference: Link to both the holding institution (P276/P1753) and the broader collection (P195) to support organizational hierarchies.
URL Persistence: Use institutional persistent identifiers (DOI, UUID) when available to ensure long-term reference stability.
Condition Documentation: Record current condition status regularly and document any conservation treatments with dates to support curatorial decision-making and insurance records.

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
Related Standards & References

CIDOC CRM v7.1.3 - Formal ontology standard for cultural heritage
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform implementation
Wikidata WikiProject Exhibitions - Community guidelines for exhibition properties
Wikidata Art Project - Guidelines for artwork documentation
Wikimedia Commons - Free media repository for artwork images
Linked Open Exhibition Project - Project repository

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
Contact & Contributions
For updates to this data model or suggestions for improvement, please contact the NFDI4Culture Linked Open Exhibition project team or contribute via GitHub.

title: Item in Exhibition Data Model
text:
Attribution
This data model documentation was created with assistance from GitHub Copilot (powered by Claude Haiku 4.5). The model was aligned against sector standards including CIDOC CRM v7.1.3, Wikibase4Research, and Wikidata WikiProject Exhibitions guidelines through AI-assisted analysis and formatting.

title: Exhibition Data Model
text:
This data model provides a standardized schema for describing exhibitions and their contextual information within the Linked Open Exhibition ecosystem. It is aligned with three major sector standards:

CIDOC CRM (Conceptual Reference Model) v7.1.3
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform
Wikidata - WikiProject Exhibitions

title: Exhibition Data Model
text:

title: Exhibition Data Model
text:
Example: Sprengel Museum Exhibition (Q138573075)
Name: Sprengel Museum Exhibition
Location: Sprengel Museum Hannover (Q510144)
Start Date: 2024-01-15
End Date: 2024-06-30
Curator: [Person Item]
Participants: Various artists
Official Website: https://www.sprengel-museum.de/
Organizer: Sprengel Museum
Country: Germany (Q183)
Language: German (Q188)
Reference: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q138573075

title: Exhibition Data Model
text:
Best Practices for Exhibition Records

Use QIDs for References: Always link to existing Wikidata items when available (Q464980 for exhibitions, institution Q-numbers, country Q-numbers, etc.)
Date Formatting: Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for all temporal data. For exhibitions spanning years, precision can be reduced to YYYY (year only).
Multilingual Titles: Where applicable, add exhibition titles in multiple languages using separate items or monolingual text fields.
Institutional Context: Link to the organizing institution’s Wikidata item (P17 for country, P664 for organizer) to enable cross-institutional exhibition discovery.
Participant Documentation: Ensure all participating artists have Wikidata items. Create items for lesser-known artists to enrich the knowledge graph.
Commons Integration: Regularly link exhibition items to Wikimedia Commons categories (P373) to support multimedia documentation and community contributions.

title: Exhibition Data Model
text:
Related Standards & References

CIDOC CRM v7.1.3 - Formal ontology standard for cultural heritage
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform implementation
Wikidata WikiProject Exhibitions - Community guidelines for exhibition properties
Wikidata Main Properties Guide - Core Wikidata documentation
Linked Open Exhibition Project - Project repository

title: Wikidata Item Profile
text:
This notebook-backed page queries Wikidata with SPARQL and renders a styled HTML profile.
To profile another item, change the item_id value in Cell 3 and re-render the site.



SELECT ?property ?propertyLabel ?value ?valueLabel WHERE {
  BIND(wd:Q138638019 AS ?item)
  ?item ?p ?statement .
  ?property wikibase:claim ?p .
  ?statement ?ps ?value .
  ?property wikibase:statementProperty ?ps .

  SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language "[AUTO_LANGUAGE],en". }
}
ORDER BY ?propertyLabel




8








  
    
    Item: Q138638019 · Statements: 8 · Generated: 2026-07-19 19:13 UTC
    Open item on Wikidata
  

  
    
      
      catalog1end time1instance of1location1main subject1official website1start time1title1
    
    
      
      
    
      
        
        
      
      
        5 Entity values
        3 Literal values

              exhibition

title: Wikidata Item Profile
Project Log
text:
Track important milestones and implementation notes here.



Initialized Quarto website source files.
Added GitHub Pages deployment workflow.
Set Quarto output directory to docs for GitHub Pages publishing.
Added notebook-backed item profile page for Wikidata item Q138547468.
Created reusable helper module wikidata_profile.py for query, fetch, and rendering logic.
Added visual summaries to the item profile page (top properties and value type breakdown).
Fixed Wikidata endpoint access by sending explicit HTTP headers in SPARQL requests.
Parameterized notebook workflow so item_id can be changed in one place.
Enabled collapsible code blocks in rendered notebook HTML.
Initialized git repository, pushed to mrchristian/prototype, and connected origin.
Fixed GitHub Actions Pages setup failure by enabling automatic Pages enablement in workflow.
Added manual workflow_dispatch deployment instructions in project documentation.




Use these steps to publish and re-publish the site to GitHub Pages.

Commit and push updates to main.
In GitHub, open Settings -> Pages.
Under Build and deployment, set Source to GitHub Actions.
Confirm the workflow file .github/workflows/publish.yml is present.
Wait for the Publish Quarto Site workflow to complete.




Open Actions in GitHub.
Select Publish Quarto Site.
Click Run workflow and choose main.





Re-check Settings -> Pages is enabled for GitHub Actions.
Re-run the workflow after enabling Pages.

title: Wikidata
text:
This section documents Wikidata workflows, queries, and references for the project.



Open the rendered profile page: Wikidata Item Profile




The profile page is generated from wikidata-item.ipynb, which:

Sets an item_id input (for example, Q138547468).
Builds a SPARQL query for all direct statements on the item.
Calls the Wikidata Query Service endpoint.
Renders the returned data as a styled HTML profile.

The helper module wikidata_profile.py contains reusable logic for querying, transforming, and rendering the data.

The helper module wikidata_profile.py contains reusable logic for querying, transforming, and rendering the data.

title: Artist Data Model
text:
Best Practices

When Creating Artist Records:

Always include: Q-identifier (Wikidata link) for interoperability
Use controlled vocabularies for professions and nationalities
Specify date precision: Use YYYY if only year is known, YYYY-MM-DD if full date available
Cross-reference authority files: Include P227 (GND) and P245 (Getty ULAN) identifiers when available
Link to Commons: Provide P373 references to media collections
Use consistent naming: Follow “Given Name” + “Family Name” convention



For Exhibition Context:

Use WikiProject Exhibitions properties to link artists to exhibitions (P1690: related work)
For performing artists: Reference WikiProject Performing arts guidelines
For visual artists: Reference WikiProject Visual arts guidelines
Ensure all date formats are ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) for data interoperability

title: DNB Exhibition Catalogue Data Model
text:
Best Practices for Catalogue Records

Use Wikidata Items: Always link to existing Wikidata items for authors (P50), publishers (P123), and organizations to enable cross-institutional discovery.
ISBN Validation: Ensure ISBN-13 numbers are valid according to the standard checksum algorithm. Use the format with hyphens (978-X-XXXX-XXXX-X) for readability.
GND Integration: Cross-reference GND identifiers from the German National Library to enhance discoverability within German cultural heritage networks.
Publication Dates: Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) where precise dates are available, or just YYYY for year-only precision.
Multilingual Titles: Where catalogues are published in multiple languages, create additional title entries using monolingual text or language-qualified items.
Cover Images: Upload catalogue cover images to Wikimedia Commons (P18) to provide visual context and enhance accessibility.
URL Persistence: Prefer institutional persistent URLs (with DOI or similar) over temporary web links when available to ensure long-term reference stability.

title: DNB Exhibition Catalogue Data Model
text:
Related Standards & References

CIDOC CRM v7.1.3 - Formal ontology standard for cultural heritage
Wikibase4Research (WB4R) - NFDI4Culture platform implementation
Wikidata WikiProject Books - Community guidelines for bibliographic properties
German National Library (DNB) - Primary source for GND identifiers
ISBN Standard - ISBN-13 validation and format standards
Linked Open Exhibition Project - Project repository
